text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Patel  
Riverside Health Centre  
144 Riverside Road, Bristol BS1 4QH  

Dear Dr Patel,                                                                                                         

James O’Connor, DOB: 22-11-1990, Hospital No: B673920 NHS No. 9083456172  
Flat 3, 27 Harcourt Avenue, Bristol, BS7 9LP  

Epilepsy 

Medication: Ongoing Levetiracetam since March 2024  

Levetiracetam 750mg twice daily (patient-confirmed current regimen) 

Levetiracetam Blood Level:  

17 September 2025: 18 µg/mL (laboratory reference range 12–46 µg/mL). Dose 750mg twice daily.  

Seizures: The patient reports no events, warnings, or episodes suggestive of seizures over the past 12 months; they describe their condition as settled with no breakthrough symptoms during this period. 

I conducted a routine follow-up via video consultation today. The patient reports excellent adherence, using a weekly pill organiser and setting phone reminders. No adverse effects from levetiracetam were noted, including no mood changes, dizziness, or somnolence. Sleep routine is consistent (7–8 hours/night), and the patient has avoided sleep deprivation and excess alcohol as previously discussed. They are not currently driving and understand to inform the DVLA if any seizure occurs in the future.

No dose adjustment is needed at present. I advised continuing the current regimen and to obtain a repeat trough level prior to the next review, or earlier if any suspected events occur. We also discussed rescue planning: the patient is aware to seek urgent care if a prolonged convulsive event occurs or if there is clustering of episodes. 

Plan:  
- Continue levetiracetam 750mg twice daily.  
- Repeat trough level in 6 months or sooner if symptomatic change.  
- Safety counselling reinforced (sleep hygiene, alcohol moderation, prompt reporting of any episodes).  
- Routine follow-up in 6–9 months via clinic or sooner if concerns arise. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: no events, warnings, or episodes suggestive of seizures over the past 12 months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "no events, warnings, or episodes suggestive of seizures over the past 12 months." This provides a clear seizure-free duration of 12 months as of the clinic date (02 October 2025). This meets the criterion of at least 6 months seizure-free, so the normalized label is "seizure free for 12 month." No other seizure frequencies are mentioned.
seizure_frequency_number: no events, warnings, or episodes suggestive of seizures over the past 12 months

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 12 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
